growth_records.cite: Jacobson et al. In Vivo Thermodynamic Analysis of Glycolysis in Clostridium thermocellum and Thermoanaerobacterium saccharolyticum Using 13C and 2H Tracers. mSystems (2020). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00736-19. [PMC7380578]
growth_records.snippet: ...pH 7 for C. thermocellum and pH 6.8 for T. saccharolyticum). ... Measured uptake and excretion fluxes for biomass, lactate, ethanol, acetate, and glucose in T. saccharolyticum. Tubes were placed in a 55°C water bath after inoculation for
growth_records.notes: T. saccharolyticum grown on glucose (5 g/L) in MTC medium, pH 6.8, 55°C, strictly anaerobic, batch, harvested mid-exponential (OD600 0.40-0.5). Full MTC recipe transcribed verbatim (glucose is the carbon source for this organism). Fluxes from COMPLETE-MFA over five 13C/2H tracer experiments are proportions of glucose uptake normalized to 100; absolute specific growth rate and absolute uptake/excretion fluxes are in supplementary Table S2 (not in fulltext XML), so no numeric mu or absolute qS/qP extractable. Secreted products explicitly listed for this organism: lactate, ethanol, acetate. Confidence low due to absent numeric rates.

up.units: g/L, fully consumed by ~90 h (409 mM)

sec.units: g/L accumulated in medium (203±2 mM) — glucose released from cellobiose accumulated extracellularly
accumulated intracellularly at fermentation cessation; extracellular values in figures only

growth_records.cite: Sharma, Bishal Dev et al. Metabolic imbalance limits fermentation in microbes engineered for high-titer ethanol production. mSystems (2026). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00074-26. [PMC13098246]
growth_records.method: not reported (time-course only; no numeric mu)
growth_records.snippet: For T. saccharolyticum, the final ethanol titer reached 949 ± 6 mM (43.7 ± 0.3 g/L), with 203 ± 2 mM (36.5 ± 0.4 g/L) of glucose accumulating in the medium. Although cellobiose was fully consumed by ~90 h, fermentation was not substrate-lim
growth_records.notes: Primary detailed fermentation (Fig 2). Cellobiose (MW 342.3) 140 g/L = 409 mM, fully consumed by ~90 h. Cellobiose gives 2 glucose; glucose-equivalents = 140 × (2×180.16/342.30) = 147.4 g/L (818 mM). Residual free glucose accumulated in medium = 36.5 g/L (203 mM), so net glucose-equiv consumed = 147.4 - 36.5 = 110.9 g/L (615 mM). Ethanol yield = 43.7/110.9 = 0.394 g/g; molar 949/615 = 1.54 mol/mol = 77% of 2.0. Substrate is cellobiose but the organism excretes free glucose (listed under secretion). No gDW/specific-rate data reported.

growth_records.medium: MTC-7 chemically defined medium + yeast extract

up.units: g/L initial loading (consumption not reported for this row)

growth_records.cite: Sharma, Bishal Dev et al. Metabolic imbalance limits fermentation in microbes engineered for high-titer ethanol production. mSystems (2026). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00074-26. [PMC13098246]
growth_records.snippet: T. saccharolyticum M1442 ... 61 g/L MTC7 140 g/L cellobiose 8.5 g/L [yeast extract] – 51 [°C] This study
growth_records.notes: Table 2 (this study): adding 8.5 g/L yeast extract to defined MTC-7 raised max ethanol titer to 61 g/L (vs 44 g/L with pH 6.0 control and no YE, and 34 g/L with neither). Trace vitamins/minerals as in MTC-7 also present (omitted here for brevity). Consumption not reported for this row.

growth_records.snippet: T. saccharolyticum M1442 ... 34 g/L MTC7 140 g/L cellobiose 0 [yeast extract] – [no pH] 51 [°C] This study
growth_records.notes: Table 2 (this study): defined MTC-7, no yeast extract, no pH control → 34 g/L ethanol (lowest of the M1442 conditions). Initial pH 6.5 set with H2SO4 per Methods but not actively controlled. Trace vitamins/minerals present (omitted for brevity). Consumption not reported.

growth_records.strain: LL1328 (M1442 Δtdk; homoethanologenic parent; derived from JW/SL-YS485, Δpta Δack Δldh adhE-G544D)

up.units: g/L (initial substrate, fully consumed by LL1328)

sec.units: g/L (highest of all strains; serves as reference baseline; absolute value in Supporting Table S2, not in main text)
mM (PFL byproduct; value in Fig 3C / Supporting Table S2)
mmol headspace H2 / L (reference baseline; nfn deletions were 2.5-3.0x higher)

mfa.note: Relative flux estimate from fermentation products; LL1328 baseline, mutants compared by Dunnett test (Fig 3E). Absolute values in figure only.
Not measured in this study; range quoted from authors' prior work (refs 49,50). Carbon recovery ~90-110% for LL1328.

yields.ethanol_yield: ~80% of theoretical maximum (quoted from prior work ref 24 for high-yield strains; not directly measured here)

growth_records.cite: Fabri, João H. T. M. et al. Adaptive evolution of electron transfer pathways in Thermoanaerobacterium saccharolyticum. Journal of Bacteriology (2026). DOI:10.1128/jb.00057-26. [PMC13277313]
growth_records.snippet: The chemically defined MTC-6 medium at pH 6.5 was used for end product and growth analyses and was prepared with 20 g/L cellobiose as the main carbon source... incubated without shaking for 7 days at 55°C... the LL1328 parental strain reach
growth_records.notes: Batch anaerobic fermentation, T=55C, pH 6.5. LL1328 is the homoethanologenic reference (parent M1442 = Δpta Δack Δldh adhE-G544D). Max OD600 in MTC-6 = 1.15 (in-text). Growth rate (µ) measured from OD600 slope between two exponential-phase points but reported only in Fig 2B, no numeric value in text (left null). Acetate ~2 mM end-point titer (low, because pta/ack deleted) — this is a TITER not a specific rate (no biomass or time-course, so qS/qP in mmol/gDW/h not derivable from text). Ethanol/formate/H2 absolute titers are in figures/Supporting Tables S2-S4 (not in accessible fulltext), so left null; only relative changes given in text. No mmol/gDW/h specific rates reported anywhere in the paper. Cellobiose 20 g/L is the sole carbon source stated; remainder of MTC-6 recipe cited from ref 23 (Tian et al.), hence medium_composition_stated=false.
